---
title: 3-Node Cluster (without Auth)
description: 3-node KRaft Kafka cluster with plaintext listeners for local development.
---

import Mermaid from '../../../../components/Mermaid.astro';

**Image:** `confluentinc/cp-kafka:7.8.0` (3 containers)  
**Ports:** `9092–9097` (external + controller), `29092–29094` (internal)  
**Data:** `kafka1_data`, `kafka2_data`, `kafka3_data` (named volumes)  
**Networks:** `kafka-net` (internal), `kafka-ui-net` (external, shared)

:::danger[Development use only]
This cluster has **no authentication**. Any client that can reach the host ports can produce and consume any topic. Do not run this on a machine accessible outside your local network.
:::

## Introduction

A 3-node KRaft Kafka cluster identical in structure to the [auth cluster](/templates/kafka/cluster-with-auth/) but with plaintext (unauthenticated) listeners. No SASL configuration is required to connect.

## When to Use

- Local development on your own machine
- Learning Kafka without credential complexity
- Running integration tests in an isolated environment

## Architecture

<Mermaid diagram={`
graph TB
    subgraph Cluster["3-node KRaft Cluster (no auth)"]
        subgraph B1["kafka1"]
            EXT1["EXTERNAL (PLAINTEXT)\n:9092"]
            INT1["INTERNAL\n:29092"]
            CTRL1["CONTROLLER\n:9093"]
        end
        subgraph B2["kafka2"]
            EXT2["EXTERNAL (PLAINTEXT)\n:9094"]
            INT2["INTERNAL\n:29093"]
            CTRL2["CONTROLLER\n:9095"]
        end
        subgraph B3["kafka3"]
            EXT3["EXTERNAL (PLAINTEXT)\n:9096"]
            INT3["INTERNAL\n:29094"]
            CTRL3["CONTROLLER\n:9097"]
        end
    end
    CTRL1 <-->|"KRaft quorum"| CTRL2
    CTRL2 <-->|"KRaft quorum"| CTRL3

    AnyClient["Any client\n(no credentials needed)"] -->|"KAFKA_HOST_IP:9092/9094/9096"| Cluster
    KafkaUI["Kafka UI\n(kafka-ui-net)"] -->|"kafka1-3:29092-29094"| Cluster
`} />

## Prerequisites

- Docker Engine 20.10+, Docker Compose v2
- `kafka-ui-net` network: `docker network create kafka-ui-net`
- `KAFKA_CLUSTER_ID` and `KAFKA_HOST_IP` must be set

## Ports

| Port | Broker | Listener | Used by |
|---|---|---|---|
| `9092` | 1 | EXTERNAL (PLAINTEXT) | External clients |
| `9093` | 1 | CONTROLLER | KRaft quorum |
| `9094` | 2 | EXTERNAL (PLAINTEXT) | External clients |
| `9095` | 2 | CONTROLLER | KRaft quorum |
| `9096` | 3 | EXTERNAL (PLAINTEXT) | External clients |
| `9097` | 3 | CONTROLLER | KRaft quorum |
| `29092` | 1 | INTERNAL | Kafka UI via kafka-ui-net |
| `29093` | 2 | INTERNAL | Kafka UI via kafka-ui-net |
| `29094` | 3 | INTERNAL | Kafka UI via kafka-ui-net |

## Configuration

```bash
cd kafka/confluent/3-node-cluster-without-auth
cp .env.example .env
```

| Variable | Default | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `KAFKA_CLUSTER_ID` | _(none)_ | **Yes** | `docker run --rm confluentinc/cp-kafka:7.8.0 kafka-storage random-uuid` |
| `KAFKA_HOST_IP` | _(none)_ | **Yes** | See [Prerequisites](/prerequisites/#kafka-finding-your-host-ip) |
| `KAFKA1_EXTERNAL_PORT` | `9092` | No | External listener port for broker 1 |
| `KAFKA1_CONTROLLER_PORT` | `9093` | No | Controller listener port for broker 1 |
| `KAFKA1_INTERNAL_PORT` | `29092` | No | Internal (kafka-ui-net) listener port for broker 1 |
| `KAFKA2_EXTERNAL_PORT` | `9094` | No | External listener port for broker 2 |
| `KAFKA2_CONTROLLER_PORT` | `9095` | No | Controller listener port for broker 2 |
| `KAFKA2_INTERNAL_PORT` | `29093` | No | Internal listener port for broker 2 |
| `KAFKA3_EXTERNAL_PORT` | `9096` | No | External listener port for broker 3 |
| `KAFKA3_CONTROLLER_PORT` | `9097` | No | Controller listener port for broker 3 |
| `KAFKA3_INTERNAL_PORT` | `29094` | No | Internal listener port for broker 3 |

## Workflow

```bash
# Generate cluster ID
docker run --rm confluentinc/cp-kafka:7.8.0 kafka-storage random-uuid

# Fill in .env
vim .env

# Start
docker compose up -d

# Verify
docker compose ps

# Produce a test message
docker compose exec kafka1 \
  kafka-console-producer \
  --bootstrap-server localhost:9092 \
  --topic test-topic

# Consume
docker compose exec kafka1 \
  kafka-console-consumer \
  --bootstrap-server localhost:9092 \
  --topic test-topic \
  --from-beginning

# Stop
docker compose down

# Stop and remove all data (topics, consumer offsets) — destructive
docker compose down -v
```

## Connecting External Clients (No Auth)

```
KAFKA_HOST_IP:9092,KAFKA_HOST_IP:9094,KAFKA_HOST_IP:9096
```

No security properties needed — connect with `security.protocol=PLAINTEXT`.

Python example:
```python
from kafka import KafkaProducer
producer = KafkaProducer(
    bootstrap_servers=['localhost:9092', 'localhost:9094', 'localhost:9096'],
)
producer.send('my-topic', b'hello world')
```

## Connecting Kafka UI

Bootstrap servers:
```
kafka1:29092,kafka2:29093,kafka3:29094
```

No authentication settings needed in Kafka UI.

## Migrating to the Auth Cluster

When you're ready to move to an authenticated setup:

1. Export any topic/consumer group configuration you've created
2. Bring down this cluster: `docker compose down -v` (or keep volumes if data is needed)
3. Follow the [auth cluster](/templates/kafka/cluster-with-auth/) setup guide

Note: The auth and no-auth clusters cannot share the same `KAFKA_CLUSTER_ID` or data volumes.

## Use Cases

**Local development:** Run Kafka on your own machine while building and testing an application, without managing SASL credentials.

**Learning Kafka:** Experiment with topics, partitions, and consumer groups without authentication getting in the way.

**Isolated integration tests:** Spin up a disposable cluster in CI or a local sandbox, produce/consume test data, then tear it down with `docker compose down -v`.
